title: Define a record
instruction:
Records are concise immutable data containers.

Run this.
successMessage: The one-liner record Point(double X, double Y) generates everything: a constructor, X/Y properties, ToString, Equals, GetHashCode.

title: Value equality
instruction:
Records compare by value, not by reference.

Run this.
successMessage: Two separate Point instances with the same values are equal. Classes use reference equality by default. Records use value equality.

title: Non-destructive mutation with with
instruction:
`with` creates a copy with some properties changed.

Run this.
expectedOutput: Config { Host = example.com, Port = 443, Debug = False }
Config { Host = localhost, Port = 443, Debug = True }
successMessage: with creates a new record, leaving the original unchanged. Immutable data structures and non-destructive mutation are functional programming patterns.

title: Record deconstruction
instruction:
Positional records support deconstruction.

Run this.
successMessage: Positional records generate a Deconstruct method. var (name, age) in people extracts each record's fields.

title: Record with custom members
instruction:
Add computed properties and methods to records.

Run this.
successMessage: Records can have methods and computed properties like regular classes. ToString is overridden to format nicely.

title: Record struct
instruction:
`record struct` is a value type record (stack-allocated for small types).

Run this.
successMessage: record struct is a value type — copied when assigned, no heap allocation for small structs. Still gets with and value equality.

title: Inheritance with records
instruction:
Records can inherit from other records.

Run this.
successMessage: Records support inheritance. Each subtype adds its own properties. ToString shows all properties including inherited ones.

title: Records as DTOs
instruction:
Records excel as Data Transfer Objects (DTOs) in APIs.

Run this.
successMessage: Records serialise to JSON cleanly. Deserialised instance equals the original because records use value equality. Perfect for API request/response models.
